markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, passage_fallback, and blocked modes with non_hybrid_reason. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: RetrievalModeEnumExpansion leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RetrievalModeEnumExpansion is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 0f78b03d; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

findings.title: passage_fallback and blocked collapse to hybrid_rag/none in the durable mode column
findings.evidence: storage_mode_of (storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs:31) maps via to_storage_str so PassageFallback->hybrid_rag, Blocked->none; a raw knowledge_retrieval_traces.retrieval_mode read shows hybrid_rag for a passage-fallback run. The distinction survives only in mode_reason/decisions.
findings.fix: Acceptable (reason recorded); if column-level fidelity matters, widen the storage enum or add a sub-mode column.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-129 (RetrievalModeEnumExpansion) defines the spec-canonical cheapest-authoritative retrieval-mode axis (none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag) plus passage_fallback/blocked planner postures and the non_hybrid_reason vocabulary, with a QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace data model whose storage-string collapse and non-hybrid-reason invariant are real, fully implemented, and proven against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL. The code matches current Master Spec v02.194 (2.3.14, 2.6.6.7.14.5) exactly.
findings: memory/retrieval_mode.rs:174-236 defines QueryRetrievalMode with all MT-required modes; to_storage_str() (207-215) collapses None|Blocked->none and HybridRag|PassageFallback->hybrid_rag; cost_rank() (219-229) implements the cheapest-first ladder; requires_non_hybrid_reason() (233-235) marks all non-hybrid modes.

memory/retrieval_mode.rs:248-264 NonHybridReason variants exactly match the current Master Spec vocabulary (02-system-architecture.md:11416: exact_identifier_known | authoritative_state_required | bounded_executor_context | freshness_uncertain | policy_disallows_hybrid_rag | user_forced_direct).

to_storage_str() five durable strings exactly match migration 0141 CHECK (0141_knowledge_context_bundles.sql:83-85: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag) and the storage enum KnowledgeRetrievalMode FromStr (knowledge.rs:1500-1513); storage_mode_of (knowledge_retrieval.rs:79-83) round-trips via parse so any drift fails closed.

knowledge_retrieval/plan.rs:199-206 QueryPlan::validate enforces the spec A0.5 invariant (02-system-architecture.md:11528: if retrieval_mode != hybrid_rag MUST populate non_hybrid_reason); storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs:103 calls validate() before any DB write so a non-hybrid trace without a reason cannot persist.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_retrieval_replay_tests.rs:132-139 persists a real QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace and replays from the durable knowledge_retrieval_traces row via replay_context_bundle_from_trace (storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs:177-325), with EventLedger receipt linkage (kernel_event_ledger, lines 587-646). Harness knowledge_pg_support.rs:26-64 uses Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (ManagedPostgres::ensure_running) or DATABASE_URL/POSTGRES_TEST_URL, returning None (legitimate skip) only when PG binaries are absent.

sub_rule_1: no reachable placeholder/deferred-live escape in any MT-129 file (only a 'folded stub intent' doc-comment at knowledge_retrieval.rs:741 referring to the MT-139..142 bridge fold, not a code stub).

Adversarial-v2 LOW (passage_fallback/blocked collapse to hybrid_rag/none in the durable mode column) is by-design with the WHY preserved in mode_reason (plan.rs:338-361) and the full replayable decisions JSONB; not a code defect.
Aside (non-blocking governance paperwork): packet cites v02.192 anchors while current is v02.194; the same section numbers resolve and content matches. Expected during repo-governance wind-down.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
